summary: Split the large item translation unit src/melee/it/it_266F.c into four subsystem-oriented files: itspawn.c, itgroundcoll.c, itdraw.c, and itdrop.c, with matching headers and build/split metadata updates. The split removed the old 1835-line TU, redistributed item spawn, ground collision, draw, and drop logic into focused TUs, moved several rodata constants to their new owning modules, and left compatibility via it_266F.h including the new headers. The decomp.dev bot reported +0.07% matched code, +2700 matched bytes, +0.14% linked code, and 65 new matches, while the apparent broken matches were mainly the old it_266F symbols disappearing as they were reassigned to the new units.
what_changed: Deleted src/melee/it/it_266F.c and replaced it with four new translation units: itspawn.c, itgroundcoll.c, itdraw.c, and itdrop.c.

Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so the old it_266F text range 0x8026C47C-0x8026F9AC became itspawn 0x8026C47C-0x8026D564, itgroundcoll 0x8026D564-0x8026EB18, itdraw 0x8026EB18-0x8026F3AC, and itdrop 0x8026F3AC-0x8026F9A0, with itcoll text now starting at 0x8026F9A0.
Updated configure.py to build itspawn, itgroundcoll, itdraw, and itdrop instead of it_266F; itgroundcoll was marked Matching while itspawn, itdraw, and itdrop were marked NonMatching.
Reduced it_266F.h from a direct declaration header into a compatibility include wrapper that includes itdraw.h, itdrop.h, itgroundcoll.h, and itspawn.h.
Added subsystem headers for the split files, including address-commented declarations such as itgroundcoll.h prototypes for it_8026D564 through it_8026EA9C and itcoll.h declaration for it_8026F9A0.

Moved constants out of the deleted TU into later TUs according to section ownership: it_803B8560 became a const Quaternion in itcoll.c, it_803B8570 and it_803B857C became const Vec3 definitions in itmaplib.c, and it_803B8588 and it_803B8598 became const Quaternion definitions in it_279C.c.
Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt for rodata and sdata2 ownership changes, including adding a local .rodata label at 0x803B85A8 and changing it_804DC6A8/it_804DC6AC to local @304/@305 symbols.
Added it_8026F9A0 in itcoll.c to reset it_804D6D18, matching the new split boundary where itcoll now starts before the prior it_8026F9AC symbol.

Made small matching-oriented edits in src/melee/it/items/itdosei.c, including include order adjustment, wrapping a condition in a temporary not_msid_4 variable, and adding itDosei_GetJObj inline to access gobj->hsd_obj.
The PR body records the intent as 'Split it_266F into itspawn, itgroundcoll, itdraw, and itdrop' and reports remaining item functions as 75 and remaining item TUs as 21.
smart_moves: Chose semantic TU names matching item responsibilities rather than keeping the opaque it_266F name: spawn, ground collision, draw, and drop.
Updated splits.txt at the exact text and data boundaries instead of only moving C files, preserving linker section ownership across the split.
Kept it_266F.h as a forwarding/compatibility header to reduce churn in existing include sites while introducing better-named subsystem headers.
Moved rodata constants to the modules that now own the relevant split ranges, avoiding dangling extern-only symbols after deleting the old TU.
Separated build matching status per new TU: one split piece, itgroundcoll.c, could be marked Matching while the other newly split pieces remained NonMatching.
Introduced small local inlines and temporary variables in itdosei.c, such as itDosei_GetJObj and not_msid_4, as tactical shape changes for matching without large behavioral edits.
Handled the off-by-one split boundary by adding it_8026F9A0 to itcoll.c and changing itcoll's split start from 0x8026F9AC to 0x8026F9A0.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a monolithic TU, update configure.py, splits.txt, symbols.txt, headers, and section-owned constants together; a source-only split is not enough.
A compatibility header can make a large TU split less disruptive: replace old declarations with includes of the new subsystem headers so existing users can migrate gradually.
Data ownership must follow linker ranges. The split caused rodata previously associated with it_266F to be reassigned to itcoll, itmaplib, it_279C, and itzako ranges.
Matching status can improve after splitting even when bot output shows 'broken matches' for the deleted unit; interpret old-TU regressions against new-TU matches.
Tiny boundary functions matter for split accuracy. Adding a small function at 0x8026F9A0 let itcoll begin at the correct new address.
In decomp matching work, include order, const definitions versus externs, local symbol scope, and small inline wrappers can affect matching and data placement.
Subsystem-oriented file names are useful decomp affordances even while functions retain address-based names such as it_8026C47C, it_8026D564, it_8026EB18, and it_8026F3AC.
naming_conventions: New item subsystem files use descriptive lowercase names with the it prefix: itspawn, itgroundcoll, itdraw, and itdrop.
Headers mirror their C files: itspawn.h, itgroundcoll.h, itdraw.h, and itdrop.h.
Existing address-based function names were preserved across the split, e.g. it_8026C47C, it_8026D564, it_8026EB18, it_8026F3AC, and it_8026F9A0.
Internal helper names in the diff used function-address prefixes plus role suffixes, such as it_8026EECC_inline_1, it_8026F3D4_check_kind, it_8026E15C_inline1, and itDosei_GetJObj.
Local symbol map entries may use generated @NNN names when a prior global-looking data symbol becomes local, as shown by @304 and @305 for sdata2 at 0x804DC6A8 and 0x804DC6AC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used precise split ranges in config/GALE01/splits.txt to carve the original it_266F object into four text ranges and redistribute sdata/sdata2/rodata.
Changed const/global data placement by defining former extern constants in their owning TUs: Quaternion and Vec3 rodata moved into itcoll.c, itmaplib.c, and it_279C.c.
Marked itgroundcoll.c as Matching while keeping itspawn.c, itdraw.c, and itdrop.c NonMatching, allowing partial progress after the TU split.

Added local inline helpers in itdraw.c and itgroundcoll.c, including it_8026EECC_inline_* and it_8026E15C_inline*/it_8026E248_inline/it_8026E32C_inline, indicating matching-sensitive extraction of repeated or compiler-shaped code.
Kept an explicit extern u64 __shr2u(u32, u32, s32) in itspawn.c for 64-bit mask shifting code involved in item pick table construction.
In itdosei.c, small shape changes such as introducing temporary variables and an inline accessor for gobj->hsd_obj were used as low-risk matching nudges.
Adjusted symbols.txt local/global scope for sdata2 constants and added a local rodata label, showing that symbol visibility annotations are part of matching after a split.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the provided PR slice.

The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 69.42% (+0.07%, +2700 bytes), linked code 41.93% (+0.14%, +5556 bytes), matched data 41.21% (+0.01%, +176 bytes), and linked data 34.95% (+0.01%, +136 bytes).

The bot reported 65 new matches in the new units and 54 broken matches in the deleted it_266F unit; this appears consistent with symbol migration from the old TU to the new split TUs rather than direct functional regressions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further split itspawn.c, itgroundcoll.c, itdraw.c, or itdrop.c.
Search for follow-up work that turns itspawn.c, itdraw.c, and itdrop.c from NonMatching to Matching after this split.
Check whether it_266F.h remains as a compatibility wrapper or is later removed after include migration.
Investigate later symbol-map changes for it_803B8560 through it_803B8598 and sdata2 @304/@305 ownership.
Look for subsequent item PRs reducing the remaining item functions count below 75 or remaining item TUs below 21.
